description:
nftables is a subsystem of the Linux kernel providing filtering and classification of network packets/datagrams/frames and is the successor to iptables. The nftables package can be removed with the following command: 
$ sudo dnf remove nftables
rationale: Running both <tt>firewalld</tt> and <tt>nftables</tt> may lead to conflict.
ocil:
Run the following command to determine if the nftables package is installed: 
$ rpm -q nftables
fixtext:
To remove the Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 package nftables run the following command:

$ sudo dnf remove nftables
title: Uninstall nftables package
